title: Native Front-End Work-Surface Shell (Rust, no webview)

markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection generated (prevents drift). GUI look/behavior is an operator-requested design doc, separate from authority.

source_control.worktree_notes: Recreated as a proper SIBLING of wt-gov-kernel with a .GOV junction/symlink to wt-gov-kernel/.GOV (verified: ReparsePoint + target). Reused branch feat/WP-KERNEL-011 at d07cffcf (main HEAD); src/frontend not yet present (created during implementation). worktree-add exited 1 ONLY on the final artifact-hygiene preflight (managed_pgdata in Handshake_Artifacts not in CANONICAL_ARTIFACT_DIRS; managed PG running on 5544); the worktree + junction were fully created before that gate. Operator to decide on the managed_pgdata hygiene allowlist.

refinement.spec_reconciliation_debt: Master Spec GUI/tech-stack sections still describe React/Tauri; reconcile via dedicated signed bundle bump (non-blocking per CX-009-VIS); see refinement.enrichment_decision.

lifecycle.signature_policy: Operator-supplied unique signature ilja190620262236 (2026-06-19) authorizing WP-KERNEL-011 activation. No ACP; folded implementation; coder/execution owner = KERNEL_BUILDER. Recorded per CX-585C (operator-provided, not fabricated).

operator_execution_order.order: Operator 2026-06-19: split GUI overhaul into two WPs (front-end shell + editors rebuild). Native Rust GUI, no webview (CX-008-VIS). As many MTs as needed, each detailed enough for a small no-context local model to execute. Minimal governance paperwork; repo governance being retired soon. Bundle ALL dependencies into one installer; everything user-owned. Author per WP/MT templates and the governance workflow.

scope.summary: Build the native Rust front-end work-surface SHELL that hosts every Handshake surface and makes them melt together, replacing the legacy React/Tauri webview UI (kept as reference only). Deliver: a native (no-webview) application skeleton; a dockable work-surface (split/resize, tabbed regions, move panes, pop-out windows, save/restore layout); the three navigation layers (top project tabs, top-right module switcher, pane-local tabs); a top menu bar + command palette + quick switcher + settings/options dialog; a context-menu system (none exists today); a bottom search rail + bottom drawer stash shelf; dark + light themes; and the AccessKit-based model-vision/steering surface so swarm agents can SEE and DRIVE every widget. Ships as a single installer that bundles all dependencies. This WP builds the SHELL + the substrate; it RE-HOMES existing surfaces only as thin placeholders and leaves real editor parity to WP-KERNEL-012. Future creative surfaces (Photoshop/Affinity/spreadsheet/engines) are designed-in extension points, NOT built here.
forbidden_paths: app/src/** (legacy React UI - reference only, do not edit)
src/backend/** (Rust backend - consume via existing API/IPC only, do not modify here)
spec_anchors: Master Spec GUI/operator-surface sections (to be reconciled to native per CX-009-VIS)
acceptance_criteria: Native app launches as a real desktop window with NO webview substrate (a webview may exist only inside a dedicated browser pane).

Work-surface supports: split horizontal/vertical, resize dividers, multiple tabs per region, move a tab/pane between regions, pop out a pane into its own OS window, merge it back, and save/restore the full layout per project.
Three navigation layers are visually distinct and behave per the GUI look/behavior doc: project tabs switch projects, module switcher switches modules, pane-local tabs switch content.
Top menu bar (FILE/EDIT/VIEW/GO/RUN/HELP), command palette, quick switcher, and settings/options dialog work and are wired to real actions.
Right-click context menus exist across every defined surface and invoke real actions.
Bottom search rail (scoped) and bottom drawer stash shelf (Agenda/Mail/Lists/Notes typed cards) work.
Dark and light themes both ship and switch at runtime.

Model-vision/steering: every interactive widget exposes a stable id + role + value + supported actions via AccessKit; an external/in-process agent can read a JSON snapshot of the full UI tree AND dispatch click/set-value/focus by stable id; a screenshot capture path exists. Proven with an automated harness (e.g. egui_kittest) AND an out-of-process check.
Concurrency: the shell is safe under N parallel swarm agents + operator co-work (no global-state clobber, attributable actions, restartable layout), proven by a concurrent harness.
QUIET: automated/model-driven operation never steals OS focus or hijacks keyboard input (focus-audit shows zero focus/Z-order theft).
Single installer builds and installs the app with ALL dependencies bundled (no system webview, no external app prerequisite, no CDN), proven by an install smoke test on a clean Windows profile.
Existing surfaces are re-homed as thin placeholders that load via the existing backend API; real parity is deferred to WP-KERNEL-012.

toolkit_decision.recommended: egui + egui_tiles (docking) + AccessKit (model surface) + egui_kittest (automation) + wgpu
toolkit_decision.recommended_rationale: Only evaluated stack with mature first-party AccessKit (the make-or-break model see/steer requirement) AND an automation harness (egui_kittest), plus IDE-class precedent (Rerun viewer on egui_tiles), single-binary Windows, and dark/light theming. Research basis: 2026-06-19 spike (4 agents).
toolkit_decision.alternative: GPUI (Zed's framework) + gpui-component (dock + 200K-line code editor + LSP + terminal). Stronger editor foundation; AccessKit landed May 2026 (younger), pre-1.0 churn, harder standalone, self-built screenshot/UIA driver.
toolkit_decision.decision_gate: WP-KERNEL-011 MT-001..004 spike locks the toolkit with evidence (AccessKit see+steer on Windows, egui_tiles/GPUI docking with layout persistence, an editor surface, single-binary build) before the heavy build. Operator may override to GPUI.

model_accessibility_contract.principle: CX-005-VIS: every GUI surface and tool is natively visible and steerable by LLM swarm agents.
requirements: Every interactive widget sets a stable AccessKit author_id derived from a logical id registry (not frame counters), plus role, label, value, and supported actions.
An in-process consumer serializes the full UI tree to JSON (id, role, name, value, actions, children) on demand - the native analog of the DOM snapshot.
An action channel accepts {op: click|set_value|focus|select|scroll, target: <author_id>, data?} and dispatches AccessKit ActionRequests.
A screenshot capture path (windows-capture/xcap) provides vision frames.
All of the above are exposed as MCP-style tools (list_widgets / click_widget / set_value / screenshot) for the swarm, bound to localhost/named-pipe with a session token.
Stable ids tie into GLOBAL-BUILD-040 stable-identifier and GLOBAL-GOVARTIFACTS-006 ID-registry policy.

dependencies_built_in_contract.requirement: CX-008-VIS: one installer, all dependencies bundled, user-owned, no external app prerequisite.
items: GUI toolkit + render engine (egui/wgpu or GPUI) vendored via lockfile.
Managed PostgreSQL binaries bundled (already managed by handshake_core).
Fonts, terminal core, syntax grammars vendored; no CDN.
Webview component (if any) only for the in-app browser pane, bundled.
Installer produces a single artifact; in-installer update channel is a designed-in extension point.

mt_plan.note: Cluster plan. Individual MT-*.json contracts are authored per the MICRO_TASK_CONTRACT_TEMPLATE and must each be executable by a small no-context local model. Authored via the governed authoring workflow.

clusters.name: Toolkit decision + native foundation
clusters.summary: Toolkit head-to-head spike (egui vs GPUI: AccessKit see+steer on Windows, docking+layout persistence, an editor surface, single-binary build) and LOCK; native app skeleton (window, render loop, no webview); dark/light theme token system; workspace crate + single-binary build + installer scaffold + bundled-deps policy.

clusters.name: Dockable work-surface
clusters.summary: Typed pane registry; horizontal/vertical split + resizable dividers; tabbed regions (move/pin/dirty/reorder, drag between regions); pop-out into OS window + merge back; per-project versioned layout persistence with last-known-good fallback + reset; integrated thin splitter/scrollbar rails.

clusters.name: Navigation layers
clusters.summary: Top project tabs (projects only, restore layout on switch); top-right module switcher (MAIN/CKC/INGEST/STAGE/LAB/STUDIO); pane-local tabs with header-title binding + type label; left activity rail + project tree + active-window quick-links + Stash + Agenda/Mail/Notes affordances.

clusters.name: Menus + commands + settings
clusters.summary: Top menu bar FILE/EDIT/VIEW/GO/RUN/HELP wired to real actions; command palette; quick switcher; settings/options dialog (theme dark/light, keybindings editor, layout presets, about).

clusters.name: Context menus
clusters.summary: Reusable context-menu infrastructure (keyboard-accessible, stable ids); per-surface context menus part 1 (tab, pane header, editor body, file/explorer row); part 2 (loom node, canvas node, source-control change, console/list rows, drawer item, status bar).

clusters.name: Bottom search + drawer
clusters.summary: Persistent bottom search rail with scopes (project/file/pane/window/stash/trace/terminal/stage/layout); bottom drawer stash shelf with Agenda/Mail/Lists/Notes typed cards; drawer item actions (stow/pin/promote/send-to-pane/copy-to-prompt/attach-evidence/convert-artifact/discard) + right-aligned + affordance.

clusters.name: Model-vision/steering (AccessKit) + swarm safety
clusters.summary: AccessKit tree with stable author_ids/roles/actions on every widget; in-process consumer + JSON UI-tree snapshot; action channel (click/set_value/focus/scroll) + screenshot capture; MCP-style swarm tool surface (localhost/pipe + token); concurrency-safe under N parallel agents + operator (leases, attributable actions, restartable layout) with a concurrent harness.

clusters.name: Shell proof + installer
clusters.summary: Automated visual + interaction proof across panes/themes/viewports (egui_kittest or equiv) + out-of-process AccessKit/UIA steer proof; focus-audit zero (HBR-QUIET); single-installer build + clean-profile install smoke with all deps bundled.

assumptions_to_attack: A native UI cannot be reliably driven by LLM agents (refute via AccessKit see+steer proof).
Immediate-mode UI cannot give stable element ids (refute via logical id registry -> AccessKit author_id).
Pop-out windows + layout persistence corrupt or fail to restore (validate snapshot versioning + last-known-good).
The toolkit choice is assumed; spike must prove the make-or-break axes before heavy build.
Bundling all dependencies into one installer is assumed feasible; install smoke on a clean profile must prove it.
minimum_controls: MT-001 toolkit spike must pass AccessKit see+steer on Windows before downstream MTs are claimed.
Every interactive widget MUST carry a stable author_id; a check fails the gate on an interactive widget with no id/actions.
Layout restore must validate before apply and keep a last-known-good fallback.
No external app / system webview / CDN as a default dependency; install smoke proves bundling.

wp_validator_pass.reviewer: INTEGRATION_VALIDATOR(acting WP_VALIDATOR) session boa3epvo9 — operator-directed retro WP-Validator pass
wp_validator_pass.method: retro WP-Validator pass (no WP Validator was used during folded KERNEL_BUILDER implementation); 31 MTs reviewed via 2-stage deep-review + adversarial-verify; bar = good code vs MT/WP/Master-Spec; repo-governance paperwork explicitly non-blocking per operator.

MT-001.one_line: Upholds PASS: all five probes exercise REAL resources (out-of-process UIA child process, egui_tiles serde round-trip with non-default 0.30/0.70 shares, egui_kittest input pipeline, real PE import-table parse, real wgpu offscreen render pass), the make-or-break RISK-7 steering link is genuinely non-fakeable (CPU angle mirror written at viewport_probe.rs:387 inside prepare(), in the same path as queue.write_buffer, only reachable when the GPU write runs; before=0.0 after=1.0 asserted), the verdict JSON honestly records egui 5/5 and is committed with an exact-pinned committed Cargo.lock, and the only two deviations (Name-match vs AutomationId, no pixel readback) are contract-authorized throw-away-spike scope routed to MT-002.

MT-002.one_line: Independent re-verification confirms PASS: the handshake-native crate honestly implements the MT-002 skeleton against real code — a real eframe/wgpu native window (no webview), a genuine non-blocking reqwest GET /health poll against the Handshake-owned backend, and a real egui_kittest AccessKit-tree query (get_by_label('Handshake') resolves the live node set via accesskit_node_builder) — with only documented, intent-preserving deviations.

MT-003.one_line: Independent re-verification confirms the first reviewer: the theme token system honestly and correctly implements the MT-003 contract with verbatim-ported dark/light palettes, a muted syntax set, graceful override/hex/rgba parsing, a REAL runtime-switch toggle (interactive egui widget, stable Id->NodeId 10, Role::Button via widget_info + Sense::click) proven end-to-end via kittest against the real HandshakeApp, and a non-vacuous no-hardcoded-Color32 invariant that I re-ran to zero matches across all 30 MTs; the rgba (6,36,17,46) deviation is egui-correct arithmetic I recomputed, not a fudge.

MT-004.one_line: Independent re-verification confirms PASS: crt-static is genuinely crate-local (root .cargo/config.toml carries ONLY target-dir, no rustflags, so RISK-1 cannot override it), the DLL audit is a real object-crate PE parse with a VCRUNTIME140 canary, the bundled Inter fonts are real OFL TrueType faces whose on-disk SHA-256 EQUALS the test's hardcoded expectations (I recomputed both), font registration drives real product install_fonts, and the WiX/build-script/policy scaffold is complete; the unbuilt-binary and absent-WiX proofs are honest contract-prescribed skips/gates, not faked or mock-only.

MT-005.one_line: Independent re-verification against the real current worktree confirms PASS: the typed pane registry keystone is honestly and correctly implemented, all 7 acceptance criteria map to real (non-tautological) passing tests, AccessKit ids are stable monotonic base-100 (not frame counters), serde-skip on last_update is correct, and the registry is the LIVE single source of truth consumed by the docked render, pop-out render, and layout persistence paths with real consumer-side AccessKit proof.

MT-006.one_line: Independent re-verification upholds PASS: the 2x2 split/divider model is a faithful, correct port of the canonical React SplitWeights grid (vertical->X/columns, horizontal->Y/rows verified against App.css:3387-3392 and App.tsx:1517-1567), clamp is applied at every mutation site plus a defensive top-of-frame clamp, and every required behavior is proven by real egui_kittest live tests (pointer drag, focus-gated keyboard, end-to-end clamped AccessKit SetValue) against the actual HandshakeApp consumer-side AccessKit tree -- no mock-only or deferred-live escape.

MT-007.one_line: Independent re-review CONFIRMS PASS: per-pane tabbed regions (close/pin/reorder/dirty + atomic cross-pane drag) are implemented with the exact contract types, logic proven correct against the contract's own example cases, and REAL LIVE egui_kittest pointer-driven drag/drop + consumer-side AccessKit proof; Spec-Realism gate is clean (no deferred-live escape, no mock-only proof of the real AccessKit resource, no #[ignore]'d required behavior).

MT-008.one_line: Independent re-verification confirms PASS: pop-out/merge-back is honestly implemented over egui multi-viewport with one PaneRegistry source of truth, real consumer-side AccessKit integration tests, all red-team/state-recovery controls present, and no deferred-live escape on the required path; the contract's literal show_viewport_deferred wording is a documented, intent-preserving deviation forced by the Send+Sync+'static bound (the contract's own red-team risk #4), not a violation.

MT-009.one_line: I OVERTURN the first reviewer's AC#4 blocker (split-weight clamp-on-restore IS effectively delivered and tested via the per-frame defensive clamp at split_layout.rs:331-334 that writes back to app state, so no collapsed pane ever renders), but I UPHOLD a narrowed FAIL on AC#3: validate() (the contract's own named validate-before-restore gate) implements only 2 of its 5 specified checks - it has no pane-completeness check, no MissingPane variant, and no active_pane/active_tab membership check, with zero tests, so a schema+version-valid-but-structurally-corrupt native blob bypasses the LKG/default fallback and is applied raw on a surface the operator flagged STATE-RECOVERY-CRITICAL.

MT-010.one_line: Independently confirmed: the integrated rail primitive (shared 4px-visual-in-8px-hit geometry, idle/hover/grab/disabled state model, live-theme-derived colors) is honestly and correctly implemented with REAL egui_kittest AccessKit end-to-end steering (SetValue/ScrollUp/ScrollDown through a genuine Harness, not an implementer mock), real divider painting routed through the shared SplitterRail with zero rect_filled, and every red-team minimum-control backed by a non-tautological test; the first reviewer's PASS_V1 stands.

MT-011.one_line: Independently confirmed PASS: the top project-tab strip honestly implements project-only switching with a real save-old/load-new layout restore through the reused MT-009 PostgreSQL-backed persistence path, stable AccessKit Tab/TabList ids proven in the live tree, no stubs/ignores on the required path, all 7 acceptance criteria met by substantive end-to-end tests.

MT-012.one_line: Independent re-verification confirms the first reviewer: the top-right six-module switcher is a verbatim, line-by-line-correct port of the React MODULE_DEFINITIONS/setModule model with fixed stable AccessKit ids (band 51..56), a sound no-op/deferred-save model, real round-trip persistence through the sanctioned LayoutPersistenceManager seam, and substantive live-tree (kittest) tests; all 7 acceptance criteria are MET and the only deviations are disclosed paperwork/path drift that the operator directive excludes as a code blocker.

MT-013.one_line: Upholding PASS_V1: MT-013 honestly delivers per-pane binding headers (live active-tab title, module/type badge, lock button) and focus-on-tab-click with stable registry-declared AccessKit ids, all proven by REAL egui_kittest live tests that mutate real HandshakeApp/PaneRegistry state through the consumer-side AccessKit tree; the only contract-vs-code tension (AC7 dot-form id) is an internal contract inconsistency the impl correctly resolved toward the MT-007 dependency's established hyphen/index form, and the higher-authority WP accessibility contract (stable, registry-derived author_id) is fully met.

MT-014.one_line: Independent re-verification UPHOLDS PASS_V1: the left activity rail is honestly and correctly implemented end-to-end (collapsible SidePanel with backward-compatible persisted drawers.project, real async HTTP project tree, bookmarks via the verified real /workspaces/:id/loom/views/pins -> Pins{blocks} route, quick links, stash/agenda/mail/notes affordances, an in-process delete bus, and a robust last-known-good layout-restore path) with no deferred-live escapes on any required path and real kittest + live-PG proof.

MT-015.one_line: Independent re-verification confirms the C4 top menu bar (FILE/EDIT/VIEW/GO/RUN/HELP + Alt mnemonics) honestly and correctly implements the MT-015 contract: every action routes into the SAME shared shell state-mutation paths the rails/keymap use, proven end-to-end by real egui_kittest harness tests against the live HandshakeApp and the live AccessKit tree, with no stubbed/faked/deferred required-behavior path.

MT-016.one_line: Independent re-review upholds PASS_V1: the command palette is a faithful, honest port of CommandPalette.tsx with a verbatim matches_query, a static OnceLock registry whose 13 enabled App commands every route into real pre-existing HandshakeApp state mutations (verified each helper exists), 13 honestly-disabled editor placeholders (no fakes/todo!/unimplemented! on any required path), and 8 live egui_kittest cases driving the REAL app tree covering open/focus/filter/arrow+Enter/Escape/Close/disabled-row/re-open-reset.

MT-017.one_line: Independent re-verification upholds PASS: the native quick switcher is a faithful port of the React Loom-graph switcher over the REAL PostgreSQL graph-search/recents REST contract, with off-thread HTTP (search, recents-load, AND recents-POST), a 150ms debounce + monotonic stale-sequence guard, recents-first ordering, a collision-checked AccessKit Dialog/SearchBox/ListBox/Option tree, and genuine end-to-end kittest proofs with no fakes on the required path.

MT-018.one_line: Independently confirmed: the settings dialog honestly implements the C4 contract; every wired control is real, persistence flows through the genuine PostgreSQL/EventLedger-backed GET/PUT /workspaces/:id/settings surface, and all state-recovery paths (load-on-open, normalize-fallback, debounced-flush-on-close, frame-start theme apply) are real and proven by non-tautological live-tree kittests; reviewer #1's PASS_V1 stands with one additional narrow non-blocking save-race concern.

MT-019.one_line: Independent re-verification confirms PASS: the reusable egui-Popup-drawn context-menu primitive honestly delivers the MT-019 intent (typed item model, builder, secondary-click open, wrapping keyboard nav with real AccessKit focus cursor, one-level submenu, Escape/click-away dismiss, stable ctx-menu.{id} MenuItem author_ids) all proven LIVE through egui_kittest reading the real AccessKit tree and real key_press dispatch — no stubs, no #[ignore]'d required behavior, no mock-only proof; the API-name divergence from the literal contract is a disclosed, downstream-consumed (MT-020 context_menu_surfaces), architecturally superior egui::Popup choice, not a code failure under the GOOD-CODE-vs-INTENT bar.

MT-020.one_line: Independently confirmed: all four WIRED context-menu surfaces (pane tab, pane header, project tab, explorer row) are honestly and correctly implemented end-to-end with real secondary_clicked()/show_on dispatch, a real off-thread rename PATCH to the VERIFIED backend route PATCH /workspaces/:id/loom/blocks/:id (handler patch_loom_block, confirmed registered in src/backend/handshake_core/src/api/loom.rs:89-93), real ctx.copy_text clipboard, focus-gated Shift+F10, live egui_kittest proofs, exact-id audits, and double-gated honestly-disabled future items; no reachable todo!/unimplemented!/panic! on the behavior path and no #[ignore] hiding required behavior — I uphold the first reviewer's PASS_V1.

MT-021.one_line: Independently confirmed: all six per-surface context menus are behavior-complete with exact contract stable ids and honest enable/disable, and the real-resource backend claims (scm.stage POST, scm.diff GET scope=worktree, canvas.remove DELETE, canvas z_index PATCH, loom.pin/favorite PATCH {pinned}/{favorite}, remove_edges DELETE) are proven on the wire via a real in-process TcpListener against backend routes that genuinely EXIST and accept the exact bodies sent (flatten/Option deserialization verified) — not a self-authored mock.

MT-022.one_line: Independently confirmed PASS: the bottom search rail honestly implements input + nine scope pills + operator-prefix parser + intent emission into a real lock-guarded Arc<Mutex> slot with stable AccessKit identities, proven end-to-end through the actual HandshakeApp via egui_kittest; search EXECUTION is the contract's own out-of-scope boundary (binds_backend_api None), not a deferred-live escape, and the parser parity micro-gaps I found are cosmetic and not load-bearing for any AC.

MT-023.one_line: Independent re-verification upholds the first reviewer: all 12 acceptance criteria map to real code and live-tree/wire-capture proof; the contract's binds_backend_api (/loom/views/table, /loom/views/calendar, content_type=list, response.total) was independently confirmed FABRICATED against handshake_core, and the coder's documented deviation to the verified surfaces (views/all?content_type=note|view_def, PUT /loom/journals/{today}) is the correct, honest MT-022 handling with no spec-realism escape.

MT-024.one_line: Upholding PASS: I independently re-verified every backend contract against handshake_core source and the 8-action drawer menu, the airtight single-call-site HBR-STOP DELETE gate, the swarm-safe intents, and the AccessKit tree are honestly built and proven by non-tautological wire-capture + live-kittest tests; the only NEW finding (attach-evidence block_id is silently dropped because ArtifactHashes is a fixed struct, not a map) and the disclosed production dead-action surface are real concerns but neither is a fake/deferred-escape nor an explicit AC failure under the code-vs-intent bar.

MT-025.one_line: Independently confirmed: every interactive widget in the REAL shell emits a stable author_id + correct role into the LIVE egui/eframe AccessKit TreeUpdate (not a mock), enforced by a real catch_unwind-proven naming gate and a collision-free single-sourced declared-id registry; the first reviewer's PASS stands.

MT-026.one_line: Upheld: collect_ui_tree_snapshot honestly projects egui 0.33's live accesskit 0.21.1 TreeUpdate (the same value MT-025 proves reaches the UIA adapter) into a full nested JSON UI tree with real safety controls and real tests; AC#4's literal 'Default'/'SetValue' strings are version-impossible/non-emitted (verified against accesskit 0.21.1's enum where the variant is 'Click' and egui drives text inputs via Focus), so reporting 'Click'/'Focus' is correct honest enumeration, not a cover for a missing action.

MT-027.one_line: Independent re-verification upholds PASS: the action channel, four-tool MCP JSON-RPC surface, constant-time HMAC auth, owner-restricted binding discovery, focus-safe Win32 screenshot adapter, and a REAL out-of-process tokio TCP server are honestly implemented, live-wired into the running HandshakeApp, and proven over a real loopback socket with non-tautological observable effects; the only #[ignore]'d tests defer a host-specific wgpu pixel grab (honest environmental GPU crash), not any faked required behavior.

MT-028.one_line: Independently confirmed: the lease registry, attribution log, per-connection McpSession wrapper, and LayoutGuard are real and correct, and the N=5 concurrency harness genuinely drives real SwarmMcpServer TCP listeners over real loopback sockets against the real live AccessKit snapshot of genuinely-registered shell widgets (shell.chrome.theme-toggle, bottom-rail.input), honoring contract INTENT over its literal swarm_mcp/pane/egui_tiles/Semaphore sketch — both deviations sanctioned by the contract's own implementation_notes; I uphold the first reviewer's PASS.

MT-029.one_line: Independently confirmed PASS: the harness drives the REAL HandshakeApp and steers it through the REAL MT-027 SwarmMcpServer over genuine TCP + Windows named-pipe with real HMAC auth (theme flip, set_value, -32001 reject all proven over the wire), the overlay accessibility-invariant runs the crate's REAL is_interactive predicate and surfaced+fixed real product author_id gaps, no stubs sit on the required path, and the only un-produced AC (pixel PNG readback) is an honest GPU-host #[ignore] with REAL render code behind it, not a fake hiding required behavior.

MT-030.one_line: Independently confirmed PASS: the MT honestly proves HBR-QUIET with a real, gaming-resistant always-on static source-scan (54 files, comment/literal-stripping lexer that is itself unit-tested, word-boundary matched, fails on any banned Win32 focus/input API, scanned>0 false-pass guard, equality-locked to the product guard's ban list) PLUS a genuinely real live Win32 SetWinEventHook(EVENT_SYSTEM_FOREGROUND) + SetWindowsHookExW(WH_KEYBOARD_LL) test that spawns the real handshake-native binary, discovers the real MT-027 swarm binding, drives 30 real swarm actions over a real TCP socket, and MEASURES (from KBDLLHOOKSTRUCT.flags+cookie, not hardcodes) zero app focus steals and zero app-injected keystrokes — with loud no-false-pass liveness gates; the #[ignore] on the live half is the correct GPU/desktop gate and hides nothing because the static gate enforces the same invariant every default run.

MT-031.one_line: Independently confirmed PASS: the installer build/stage/package, runtime exe-relative bundle self-check, clean-profile launch smoke, real-PE WebView2 import check, and bundled-deps audit are all real (genuine release-native crt-static binary built, staged, launched, and object-crate PE-parsed; real zip artifact; real exe-relative bundled-postgres discovery in managed_postgres.rs), and the sole un-met literal bound (>=10MB compressed) is the contract's OWN RISK-031-08 postgres-deferred fallback, honestly recorded with postgres_deferred:true in the machine-readable report.
